item.title: Issue #2651 created
item.description:
Editorial, for/let: range variables

In 4.13.2 For Clause of the current XQuery spec, the term “range variable” is used, but not defined:

When a ForItemBinding is used (that is, when none of the keywords member, key, or value is used), the range variable is bound in turn to each item in the binding collection, which is treated as a sequence of items. … (that is, when the keyword member is used), the range variable … …the key range variable (if present) …the value range variable (if present) …

The term seems to have been pasted from the 4.13.3 Let Clause paragraph…

  1. In a LetValueBinding such as let $V as T := EXPR : a. The variable V is declared as a range variable.

…but I believe a proper definition is missing also here (at least to me, it is not clear what kind of “range” is supposed to be addressed).

Maybe it would be sufficient to talk about the “variable of the let/for clause”, or the “positional/key/value/member variable”?
